title: Ceropegia calosticta
intro: Ceropegia calosticta, commonly known as the 'Zebra's Hood' or 'Calosticta', is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Ceropegia calosticta is used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia calosticta include alkaloids such as calostictine and ceropegine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Ceropegia calosticta is often prepared into a powder or decoction, as well as an oil extract, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Ceropegia calosticta is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. It belongs to the genus Ceropegia, which includes about 130 species of flowering plants. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Africa and is often cultivated for its unique and striking flowers. Ceropegia calosticta is a succulent, meaning it has adapted to store water in its leaves and stems, allowing it to thrive in dry environments. This adaptation enables it to grow in areas with limited rainfall, making it a popular choice for xeriscaping.
